paragraph: Alpine's single-family market is defined by scarcity and patience. Only five homes sold over the last six months, with a median price of $4.5 million and a sale-to-list ratio of 0.96, meaning homes are selling slightly below asking. The median time on market was 101 days, which tells you that even at the highest price points in Bergen County, sellers aren't moving inventory quickly. One home did close in 14 days, but that's an outlier. The range is wide, from $4 million to $7.3 million, reflecting the estate-lot variability typical of Alpine. With so few transactions, you're not in a market where momentum or seasonal patterns matter much. You're in a market where the right buyer for the right property eventually appears, and that can take time. If you're considering Alpine, expect to price competitively and be prepared for a longer holding period.
